title: antennaria parvifolia f. brunnea
intro: Antennaria parvifolia f. brunnea is a plant species belonging to the genus Antennaria, commonly known as the small-leaved rosy marjoram or low silver leaf. This perennial herb is native to North America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and enhanced immune function. Antennaria parvifolia f. brunnea can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for internal use or applied topically as a poultice or salve.
